response:
To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a matrix $ A $, we compute the determinant of the matrix $ A - \lambda I $, where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $, and then form the polynomial $ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) $.

Given matrix:

$$
A = 
\begin{bmatrix}
12 & -1 & -12 & 9 \\
-13 & -7 & 13 & 2 \\
-3 & -3 & 13 & -14 \\
-13 & 14 & 3 & -10
\end{bmatrix}
$$

We compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $:

$$
A - \lambda I = 
\begin{bmatrix}
12 - \lambda & -1 & -12 & 9 \\
-13 & -7 - \lambda & 13 & 2 \\
-3 & -3 & 13 - \lambda & -14 \\
-13 & 14 & 3 & -10 - \lambda
\end{bmatrix}
$$

Now, we compute the determinant of this 4×4 matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use cofactor expansion or row operations

Rather than computing the determinant directly, we can use a computational tool or perform the expansion step-by-step.

Let’s compute the determinant using cofactor expansion along the first row:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = 
\begin{vmatrix}
12 - \lambda & -1 & -12 & 9 \\
-13 & -7 - \lambda & 13 & 2 \\
-3 & -3 & 13 - \lambda & -14 \\
-13 & 14 & 3 & -10 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

We will now compute this determinant step-by-step using cofactor expansion.

---

### Step 2: Expand along the first row

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = 
(12 - \lambda) \cdot 
\begin{vmatrix}
-7 - \lambda & 13 & 2 \\
-3 & 13 - \lambda & -14 \\
14 & 3 & -10 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
